name: Fluentd
what_it_does: Open-source log collector and aggregator that unifies data collection from multiple sources and routes to multiple outputs via a plugin ecosystem.
use_cases: Collect logs from 100+ microservices and route to Elasticsearch and S3 simultaneously
Transform log formats (JSON, syslog, CSV) before forwarding to downstream systems
Aggregate metrics and logs from Kubernetes pods using fluentd DaemonSet
Buffer and retry log delivery to handle downstream outages without data loss
Parse multi-line stack traces and structured log events for search indexing
not_for: Real-time stream processing with sub-100ms latency requirements
best_when: Building centralized log pipelines that need 500+ community plugins for sources and outputs.
avoid_when: You need low-overhead log shipping with minimal configuration — use Fluent Bit instead.

auth.notes: Self-hosted daemon; authentication handled per-output plugin (e.g., Elasticsearch auth, S3 credentials)

pricing.notes: Free self-hosted; Calyptia offers managed Fluentd with SLA

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; throughput depends on hardware and plugin configuration

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors logged to stderr; config validation catches syntax errors at startup
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Buffering with file-backed buffer ensures at-least-once delivery
known_agent_gotchas: Ruby GIL limits CPU parallelism — use multiple processes for high-throughput workloads
Plugin compatibility varies by fluentd major version — check gem compatibility before upgrading
File buffer can fill disk if downstream is unavailable — set chunk_limit_size and total_limit_size
Multi-line log parsing requires careful regex — incomplete patterns cause dropped events
Memory usage spikes during buffer flush — tune flush_interval and chunk sizes

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable per plugin; secrets in config file (recommend env vars or secrets manager)
